channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: This money-saving emporium is like Facebook Marketplace for gift cards
item.description: You don’t have to be an avid reader of restaurant industry trade publications—though I can attest that they are oddly fascinating—to realize that everything’s getting more expensive. The good news is that there’s an easy way to counteract those rising menu prices.
item.dc:creator: Jared Newman, Fast Company

item.title: The end of VPN in Eastern Europe? This massive new subsea cable project looks to bypass Russia entirely - so is this a new age for Internet safety
item.description: Kardesa project seeks safer routes for global data, but true digital independence remains tangled in geopolitics and regional instability.
item.dc:creator: Efosa Udinmwen, TechRadar

item.title: About 4 in 10 worried about environmental impacts of AI: Survey
item.description: A new poll shows growing concerns among citizens over the potential environmental effects caused by artificial intelligence. The Associated Press-NORC Center poll, released Thursday, found about 4 in 10 U.S.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: CIOs, be ready for agentic AI
item.description: CIOs are staring down a paradox. Employees are moving faster than ever with agentic AI. They’re using tools like Copilot, ChatGPT and embedded SaaS agents to build automations and streamline workflows on their own.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Python has a friend in Rust
item.description: Python 3.14 is here, and it’s bursting with goodies! Plus, Rust is making it easier to redistribute Python apps, PDM is simplifying Python package management, and Java is challenging Python for developing AI applications.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Next.js 16 features explicit caching, AI-powered debugging
item.description: Vercel’s React framework for building full-stack web applications gets an upgrade in Next.js 16, with more explicit caching and AI-based debugging, among other features. Announced October 21, Next.js 16 now is generally available, with installation instructions available at nextjs.org.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: 5 big updates coming to the ChatGPT Atlas browser
item.description: OpenAI is rushing out quality-of-life upgrades for ChatGPT Atlas that promise to make the AI-powered browser better.
item.dc:creator: Eric Hal Schwartz, TechRadar

item.title: Parents claim OpenAI weakened chatbot anti-suicide protections just before teen’s death
item.description: OpenAI's launch of its pioneering ChatGPT sparked a global AI craze that has sparked hundreds of billions of dollars in investments in Silicon Valley technology companies, and raised concerns it will be used for harms ranging from rampant unemployment to terrorism.
item.dc:creator: Ethan Baron, The Mercury News

item.title: Rivian mobility spinoff readies $4,500 e-bike as first product
item.description: Also, the micromobility startup spun out of Rivian Automotive Inc., has unveiled its first consumer product: an electric bike that will cost up to $4,500.
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg, The Mercury News

item.title: Musk's rollercoaster year: From boycotts and plunging earnings to a potential trillion-dollar payday
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — If someone left a government job with a black eye, literally, ran a company with shrinking profits, and suddenly had federal investigators crawling over their business, you might say they’re having a bad year. But most people are not Elon Musk.
item.dc:creator: BERNARD CONDON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: OpenAI launches Atlas browser to compete with Google Chrome
item.description: OpenAI introduced its own web browser, Atlas, on Tuesday, putting the ChatGPT maker in direct competition with Google as more internet users rely on artificial intelligence to answer their questions.
item.dc:creator: MATT O'BRIEN and MICHAEL LIEDTKE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: AI helps IT teams resolve incidents faster, report finds
item.description: New data suggests that generative artificial intelligence (genAI) is delivering measurable benefits for IT organizations and their IT Service Management (ITSM) efforts.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Meta cutting 600 AI jobs even as it continues to hire more for its superintelligence lab
item.description: MENLO PARK, Calif. (AP) — Meta Platforms is cutting roughly 600 artificial intelligence jobs even as it continues to hire more workers for its superintelligence lab, the company confirmed on Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Google and Apple face extra UK scrutiny over 'strategic' role in mobile platforms
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Britain’s antitrust watchdog on Wednesday targeted Google and Apple for their “strategic” roles in mobile ecosystems, opening the door for regulators to impose changes to their business practices to improve competition.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Florida test scores rise, attendance improves after cellphone ban
item.description: A new study finds that Florida test scores rose and attendance improved after a school cellphone ban, although short-term disciplinary problems also arose in the state.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, The Hill

item.title: Storage constraints add to AI data center bottleneck
item.description: After GPUs, storage capacity has emerged as the next major constraint for AI data centers. Hard-drive lead times are ballooning to more than a year, and enterprise flash storage is also expected to see shortages and price increases, experts say.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Call to ban AI superintelligence could redraw the global tech race between the US and China
item.description: More than 850 prominent figures have called for a prohibition on developing AI superintelligence, a move that could reshape enterprise AI investments and intensify the US-China technology race if adopted.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Netflix blames tax dispute in Brazil for rare quarterly earnings letdown
item.description: Netflix missed the earnings target set by stock market analysts during the video streamer’s latest quarter, a letdown that the company blamed on a tax dispute in Brazil. The results announced Tuesday broke Netflix's six-quarter streak of posting a profit that eclipsed analysts' projections.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL LIEDTKE, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: OPM renews effort to consolidate 119 HR systems into one
item.description: The Office of Personnel Management is looking to implement a commercial software-as-a-service human capital management system for all agencies by July 4, 2027. The post OPM renews effort to consolidate 119 HR systems into one first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Jason Miller, Federal News Network

item.title: AI jobs that pay $200K or more
item.description: Instead of fearing what comes next with artificial intelligence, think outside the box. Here are high-earning AI jobs that don't require a computer science degree.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Accelerate together
item.description: Is Agentic AI Your New Cyber Wingman? The post Accelerate together — Secure by AI first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: wfedstaff, Federal News Network

item.title: 2025 global network outage report and internet health check
item.description: The reliability of services delivered by ISPs, cloud providers and conferencing services is critical for enterprise organizations.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: IBM signs up Groq for speedy AI inferencing option
item.description: IBM has teamed up with Groq to offer enterprise customers a reliable, cost-effective way to speed AI inferencing applications.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Rude ChatGPT prompts, better answers? What the data says
item.description: OpenAI CEO Sam Altman says polite words like "please" and "thank you" cost millions annually, while direct prompts may improve ChatGPT accuracy by several points.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Big Tech is paying millions to train teachers on AI, in a push to bring chatbots into classrooms
item.description: SAN ANTONIO (AP) — On a scorching hot Saturday in San Antonio, dozens of teachers traded a day off for a glimpse of the future. The topic of the day’s workshop: enhancing instruction with artificial intelligence.
item.dc:creator: JOCELYN GECKER, Associated Press, KRON4
